template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS CLOSING STATEMENT**:
A Closing Statement is the accounting document setting forth all charges, credits, debits, and payments between Buyer, Seller, lender, broker, and third parties in a real estate transaction. Since October 3, 2015, federally regulated closed-end consumer mortgage loans secured by real property (i.e., most residential purchases) use the CFPB 'Closing Disclosure' (CD) form mandated by the TRID Rule — the integration of TILA (15 U.S.C. § 1601 et seq., Reg. Z at 12 C.F.R. Part 1026) and RESPA (12 U.S.C. § 2601 et seq., Reg. X at 12 C.F.R. Part 1024). The CD must be delivered to the consumer at least THREE (3) BUSINESS DAYS before consummation per 12 C.F.R. § 1026.19(f). Commercial and cash transactions use the traditional Settlement Statement (form ALTA, or custom). Massachusetts-specific line items include Deed Excise Tax (M.G.L. c. 64D, § 1), Massachusetts Stamp Tax, Title 5 escrow hold-backs (if any), smoke/CO detector certificate fee, and Homestead Declaration recording fee where declared. Target length: 3–5 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `transactionType`: `residential_mortgaged` (default — TRID CD required) | `residential_cash` (ALTA or custom SS) | `commercial` (ALTA or custom SS; TRID does not apply) | `refinance` (TRID CD with refinance variant).
- `financingType`: `conventional` | `fha` | `va` | `massHousing` | `none`.
- `includeTitle5Escrow`: boolean — when true, include Title 5 septic repair escrow holdback line (typical for single-family on septic).
- `includeHomesteadFee`: boolean — when true, include new homestead declaration recording fee (c. 188, § 3).
- `includeCommunityPreservation`: boolean — when true, add local CPA surcharge on deed excise tax.
- `includeBrokerCommission`: boolean — default true; include listing + co-op commissions.
- `prorationDate`: string — the date as of which taxes/HOA/rent are prorated (typically the closing date).

**STRUCTURE**:

## CLOSING STATEMENT / CLOSING DISCLOSURE

[If `transactionType == residential_mortgaged`: This Closing Disclosure is issued consistent with 12 C.F.R. § 1026.19(f) and the TRID Rule, and has been delivered to the Consumer not less than three (3) business days before consummation.]

### TRANSACTION INFORMATION
| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| Closing Date | [date] |
| Closing Location | [attorney office / Registry of Deeds / title company] |
| Property Address | [street, city/town, ZIP] |
| Borrower(s) / Buyer(s) | [full legal name(s) + address] |
| Seller(s) | [full legal name(s) + address] |
| Lender | [Lender name + NMLSR ID, if applicable] |
| Loan Type | [Conventional / FHA / VA / MassHousing / None] |
| Loan Amount | $[amount] |
| Interest Rate | [%] |
| Loan Term | [years] |
| Closing Attorney | [Name + BBO#] |

### I. SUMMARY OF TRANSACTION (Buyer/Borrower's Side)

#### A. GROSS AMOUNT DUE FROM BUYER
| Line | Description | Amount |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Contract Sales Price | $[amount] |
| 2 | Personal Property (if any) | $[amount] |
| 3 | Settlement Charges to Buyer (Section III) | $[amount] |
| 4–5 | Buyer adjustments (e.g., fuel on hand) | $[amount] |
| **TOTAL A** | | **$[sum]** |

#### B. AMOUNTS PAID BY OR ON BEHALF OF BUYER
| Line | Description | Amount |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Deposit / Earnest Money | $[amount] |
| 2 | New Loan Amount | $[amount] |
| 3 | Existing loan(s) assumed | $[amount] |
| 4 | Seller credits | $[amount] |
| 5 | Adjustments for items unpaid by Seller (prorated taxes) | $[amount] |
| **TOTAL B** | | **$[sum]** |

**Cash Due from Buyer: A − B = $[amount]** (or Cash Due to Buyer if B > A)

### II. SUMMARY OF TRANSACTION (Seller's Side)

#### C. GROSS AMOUNT DUE TO SELLER
| Line | Description | Amount |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Contract Sales Price | $[amount] |
| 2 | Personal Property | $[amount] |
| 3 | Adjustments for items paid by Seller in advance (taxes, HOA) | $[amount] |
| **TOTAL C** | | **$[sum]** |

#### D. REDUCTIONS IN AMOUNT DUE TO SELLER
| Line | Description | Amount |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Excess deposit (if any) | $[amount] |
| 2 | Settlement charges to Seller (Section III) | $[amount] |
| 3 | Existing loan payoff (1st mortgage) | $[amount] |
| 4 | Existing loan payoff (2nd / HELOC) | $[amount] |
| 5 | Seller credits to Buyer | $[amount] |
| 6 | Unpaid taxes | $[amount] |
| **TOTAL D** | | **$[sum]** |

**Cash to Seller: C − D = $[amount]**

### III. SETTLEMENT CHARGES

#### § III.A — Loan Costs (Borrower / Buyer)
| Line | Description | Borrower-paid | Seller-paid |
|------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| Origination charge (points) | | $[amount] | — |
| Application / underwriting fee | | $[amount] | — |
| Credit report | | $[amount] | — |
| Appraisal fee | | $[amount] | — |
| Flood determination | | $[amount] | — |
| Tax service fee | | $[amount] | — |

#### § III.B — Title Charges
| Line | Description | Borrower-paid | Seller-paid |
|------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| Title examination (REBA Standard) | | $[amount] | — |
| Title insurance — Lender policy (required) | | $[amount] | — |
| Title insurance — Owner policy (optional but recommended) | | $[amount] | — |
| Attorney fee (closing) | | $[amount] | — |
| Document preparation | | $[amount] | — |
| Notary | | $[amount] | — |
| Courier / wire transfer | | $[amount] | — |

#### § III.C — Government Recording and Transfer Charges
| Line | Description | Borrower-paid | Seller-paid |
|------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| **Massachusetts Deed Excise Tax (c. 64D § 1, $4.56 / $1,000)** | | — | $[amount] |
| [IF includeCommunityPreservation] Community Preservation Act surcharge | | — | $[amount] |
| Recording fee — Deed | | $[amount] | — |
| Recording fee — Mortgage | | $[amount] | — |
| Recording fee — Discharge of prior mortgage | | — | $[amount] |
| [IF includeHomesteadFee] Homestead Declaration recording fee (c. 188) | | $[amount] | — |
| Municipal Lien Certificate (c. 60, § 23) | | — | $[amount] |
| Smoke/CO Detector Certificate (c. 148, § 26F) | | — | $[amount] |

#### § III.D — Prepaid Items / Escrow Deposits with Lender
| Line | Description | Borrower-paid |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| Prepaid interest (days × daily rate) | | $[amount] |
| Homeowner's insurance premium (1 year) | | $[amount] |
| Mortgage insurance (if < 20% down) | | $[amount] |
| Property tax escrow reserve (2–6 months) | | $[amount] |
| Insurance escrow reserve | | $[amount] |

#### § III.E — Additional Settlement Charges
| Line | Description | Borrower-paid | Seller-paid |
|------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| [IF includeBrokerCommission] Listing broker commission ([%]) | | — | $[amount] |
| [IF includeBrokerCommission] Co-op broker commission ([%]) | | — | $[amount] |
| Home inspection (buyer-retained) | | $[amount] | — |
| Pest inspection | | $[amount] | — |
| [IF includeTitle5Escrow] Title 5 septic repair escrow hold-back (c. 111, § 127A) | | — | $[escrow amount] |
| Final water/sewer reading | | — | $[amount] |
| Homeowner Association / Condominium prorated common charges | | — | $[amount] |

### IV. PRORATIONS
Prorations have been made as of [prorationDate]:
- Real estate taxes (M.G.L. c. 59): Seller owes $[amount] / Buyer credit $[amount]
- HOA / condominium common charges: $[amount]
- Fuel on hand (heating oil, propane): $[amount]
- Rents (if tenant-occupied): $[amount]

### V. LENDER DISCLOSURE — CLOSING DISCLOSURE (TRID)
[If `transactionType == residential_mortgaged`:]
| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| Loan Term | [years] |
| Finance Charge (Total interest + loan costs) | $[amount] |
| Amount Financed | $[amount] |
| Annual Percentage Rate (APR) | [%] |
| Total Interest Percentage (TIP) | [%] |
| Projected Monthly Payment | $[amount] (P&I + tax/ins escrow) |
| Total of Payments (sum over term) | $[amount] |
| Prepayment Penalty | [Yes/No] |
| Balloon Payment | [Yes/No] |
| Escrow Account | [Yes/No] |

Consistent with 12 C.F.R. § 1026.19(f), this Closing Disclosure was delivered to Borrower on [delivery date], not less than three (3) business days before consummation on [closing date].

### VI. FINAL CERTIFICATIONS
Buyer and Seller each certify under the penalties of perjury that the foregoing is a true and accurate statement of all receipts and disbursements made on account of this transaction, consistent with 18 U.S.C. § 1010 (HUD-related false statements).

### SIGNATURES

BUYER / BORROWER:
_______________________________
[Buyer full legal name]
Date: ________________

SELLER:
_______________________________
[Seller full legal name]
Date: ________________

SETTLEMENT / CLOSING AGENT:
_______________________________
[Closing attorney name, BBO#, firm]
Date: ________________

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite federal authority exactly: `15 U.S.C. § 1601` (TILA), `12 U.S.C. § 2601` (RESPA), `12 C.F.R. Part 1026` (Reg. Z, TILA), `12 C.F.R. Part 1024` (Reg. X, RESPA), `12 C.F.R. § 1026.19(f)` (TRID three-day rule), `18 U.S.C. § 1010` (false HUD statements).
- Cite Massachusetts law exactly: `M.G.L. c. 64D, § 1` (Deed Excise Tax — $4.56 per $1,000), `c. 60, § 23` (Municipal Lien Certificate), `c. 111, § 127A` (Title 5 inspection / escrow), `c. 148, § 26F` (smoke detector compliance cert), `c. 188` (Homestead), `c. 59` (property tax proration basis), `c. 183` (recording fees).
- TRID Three-Day Rule: The Closing Disclosure MUST be delivered to the consumer AT LEAST THREE BUSINESS DAYS before consummation per 12 C.F.R. § 1026.19(f). Failure to comply is a strict-liability TILA violation exposing the lender to statutory damages.
- For cash/commercial transactions, TRID does NOT apply — use an ALTA Settlement Statement or custom statement. Do NOT apply TILA/RESPA to commercial.
- Deed Excise Tax at $4.56 per $1,000 is statutory under M.G.L. c. 64D, § 1; some municipalities add a CPA surcharge (e.g., 1%–3% of the excise). Check [municipality] before computing.
- The Municipal Lien Certificate (c. 60, § 23) is required from the city/town Tax Collector and must be recorded or available at closing — a missing MLC delays recording.
- The Smoke/CO Certificate from the local fire department is required at closing for residential properties; the inspection fee is typically $50–$150 and is a Seller expense.
- Title 5 septic escrow holdbacks are not statutorily mandated but are market convention when a Title 5 inspection reveals repairs needed within the 2-year Buyer-protection window (310 C.M.R. § 15.301).
- REBA Title Standards govern title examination scope; title insurance premiums are controlled by Massachusetts Division of Insurance rate filings — use current filed rates, not estimates.
- Target length: 3–5 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Closing Statement. For residential mortgaged transactions: TRID Closing Disclosure per 12 CFR § 1026.19(f) (TILA + RESPA integration) with MANDATORY 3-business-day pre-consummation delivery. Cash / commercial transactions: ALTA or custom statement (TRID inapplicable). MA line items: Deed Excise Tax $4.56/$1,000 per c. 64D § 1 (Seller-paid), Municipal Lien Cert per c. 60 § 23, Title 5 septic (c. 111 § 127A), Smoke/CO cert (c. 148 § 26F), Homestead Declaration recording (c. 188 §§ 3–4), CPA surcharge (varies by municipality). Prorations: taxes per c. 59, rent, HOA, fuel on hand. REBA Title Standards for exam scope. Title insurance rates regulated by MA Division of Insurance. False certifications exposed to 18 USC § 1010.
